title: caltha palustris var. typica
intro: Caltha palustris var. typica, also known as marsh marigold, is a perennial flowering plant that belongs to the buttercup family (Ranunculaceae). Traditionally, the plant has been used in herbal medicine for its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antiviral properties, which can be beneficial in treating various health issues. The medicinal properties of marsh marigold can help in reducing inflammation, relieving respiratory issues, and supporting the immune system, thereby providing health benefits such as improved digestion, reduced fever, and enhanced wound healing. Herbal preparations of marsh marigold can be made into teas, tinctures, or infusions, and it is often combined with other herbs to enhance its medicinal effects.
